message:
fix(safety): ionice class guard, safe secret zeroization, field projection refactor

daemon/resource.rs — Reject ionice classes > 3 instead of passing
invalid values to the ionice command.  Linux supports classes 0–3
(none, realtime, best-effort, idle); anything else is undefined
behavior.  Adds a test asserting class 4 is rejected.

pages/qr.rs — Remove unsafe block in RecoverySecret::drop and
RecoveryArtifacts::drop.  The old code used as_bytes_mut() via unsafe
to zeroize String contents in-place.  The new implementation uses
std::mem::take().into_bytes() to move the String into a Vec<u8>,
zeroize it, then drop — achieving the same secure erasure without
unsafe.  Also extends RecoveryArtifacts to zeroize qr_png and qr_svg
payloads that may contain sensitive material.

pages/redact.rs — Switch test config construction from field-by-field
mutation to struct initialization with ..Default::default(), which is
more idiomatic and ensures new fields are explicitly considered.

lib.rs — Replace the serialize-then-lookup pattern in filter_hit_fields
with a direct projected_hit_field_value() match that avoids serializing
the entire SearchHit when only a subset of fields is requested.  Also
add an estimate_tokens parameter to clamp_hits_to_budget to skip token
estimation when neither --max-tokens nor --meta nor JSONL meta emission
needs it, avoiding unnecessary serialization of the full hit vector.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
